title: SME review signoff record (Phase 5.2)
description: Machine-readable sign-off entry recording that a qualified subject-matter expert has reviewed a UC (or a pack of UCs) for technical correctness against the authoring data source and for regulatory-evidence acceptability. Distinct from the Phase 4.5a peer review (engineering rubric) and the Phase 4.5b legal review (regulatory claim surface). See docs/sme-review-guide.md for the full process.

$schema.description: Optional IDE-tooling hint pointing to this schema.

generated_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last time this file was updated (hand-editing is fine; the value is informational).

baseline_commit.description: Short or long SHA of the commit that introduced this file. SME-review gating applies to commits AFTER this baseline; earlier tier-1 content is grandfathered (see docs/sme-review-guide.md §7).

signoffs.description: One record per PR (or direct commit) that passed the SME-review gate. Append-only — never rewrite history.

pr.description: GitHub PR number with leading '#', or the literal string 'direct-commit' for landings that bypass a PR (discouraged).

date.description: ISO-8601 date (UTC) the SME signed off.

commit.description: Short or long SHA of the commit the SME reviewed (the final commit after any requested revisions).

reviewer.description: SME's name and firm/role. Full name is intentional — the signoff file is public and auditors need an attributable review chain. Reviewers may redact to firm + role if they prefer not to be named (see docs/sme-review-guide.md §5).

reviewerRole.description: splunk-engineer: professional-services / customer-success Splunk engineer who has deployed the data source. regulatory-auditor: qualified auditor (CISA, CIPP/E, QSA, HITRUST CCSFP, ISO 27001 Lead Auditor, etc.). security-architect: enterprise security architect validating detection logic. industry-sme: industry-specific expert (clinician for HIPAA, CFO/controller for SOX-ITGC, power-system engineer for NERC CIP). internal-review-board: multi-member internal panel (used when no single SME covers the scope). See docs/sme-review-guide.md §2.

credentials.description: Optional list of recognised credentials (e.g. 'QSA', 'CIPP/E', 'CISA', 'Splunk Core Certified Consultant'). Serves as auditor-facing evidence of competency. Recommended for reviewerRole='regulatory-auditor'.

ucs.description: UC IDs reviewed. Minimum one entry — an SME signoff without UC scope is meaningless (SME review is always anchored to a concrete UC).

regulations.description: Tier-1 regulations in scope for the SME review. SME review is mandatory whenever a UC claims a tier-1 clause with assurance='full' OR carries a controlTest that cites SPL for a tier-1 audit-evidence clause (see docs/sme-review-guide.md §1).

fixtures.description: Optional list of sample-data fixture paths the SME verified (e.g. 'sample-data/uc-22.14.3/positive.json'). Populated when the SME replayed the fixture against the SPL and confirmed the expected polarity.

evidencePacks.description: Optional list of evidence-pack paths reviewed (e.g. 'docs/evidence-packs/hipaa.md'). Populated when the SME validated the evidence-pack claims against their industry knowledge of what an auditor would accept.

outcome.description: See docs/sme-review-guide.md §3 for the effect of each outcome on the PR.

revisionsRequested.description: REQUIRED when outcome is 'approved-with-revisions'. One string per revision the SME required before sign-off. Each entry MUST describe the before/after (e.g. 'UC-22.14.3 SPL changed from base search `index=main` to `index=wineventlog EventCode=4625 OR EventCode=4740` to remove the acknowledged FP on Windows lockout events').

caveats.description: REQUIRED when outcome is 'conditional'. Each caveat is propagated into an smeCaveat field on the relevant UC sidecar — typically field-extraction prerequisites, data-source version constraints, or industry-scope limitations (e.g. 'Only applies to deployments where Windows Event Forwarding is enabled; legacy wmic collection will miss the event in ~8% of cases').

rejectionReason.description: REQUIRED when outcome is 'rejected'. Explain why the UC cannot land as-authored (factually wrong SPL, data source that does not produce the claimed fields, clause cited at an unattainable assurance level, etc.).

fixtureReplayResult.description: Optional structured record of the SME's fixture replay. When present, it MUST be self-consistent: 'replayed=false' implies 'positiveDetected' and 'negativeSilent' are not decisive, and the checks.splCorrectness result should therefore be 'n/a' rather than 'pass'. When 'replayed=true', both 'positiveDetected' and 'negativeSilent' are expected to be populated; any mismatch with checks.splCorrectness is flagged by the audit script. Keep this open-ended enough to record 'close-but-not-exact' polarity where the UC's intended detection fires in a compensating way.

notes.description: Free-text reviewer note. Typical content: data-source version tested, Splunk release tested against, industry-specific context (e.g. 'Reviewed against an Epic EHR deployment on Windows Server 2022; HL7 MSH segment parsing relies on the default Epic HL7 modular input and falls back to the TA-hl7 if the input is absent.'), or notes on the difference between the intended detection and what the fixture actually exercised.

checkResult.description: pass = SME verified. fail = rubric point failed and was fixed before merge (rationale required in 'notes' or 'revisionsRequested'). n/a = rubric point does not apply (e.g. 'falsePositiveAssessment' is 'n/a' when the UC is a satisfies-only record-keeping control with no detection SPL).
